questions.question: Define covariance and Pearson correlation coefficient. Compute Pearson's r for the data: X = [2, 4, 6, 8] and Y = [3, 5, 8, 10].
keyPoints: Covariance formula and interpretation
Pearson r formula: r = Cov(X,Y) / (σx * σy)
Step-by-step computation of means, deviations, covariance
Final r value and interpretation of strength

questions.question: Explain the difference between correlation and causation with suitable examples. Why is this distinction important in machine learning?
keyPoints: Definition of correlation as statistical association
Definition of causation as direct influence
Example: ice cream sales and drowning (confounding variable)
Impact on ML: spurious features, model robustness
Confounding variables explanation

questions.question: Compare Pearson and Spearman correlation coefficients. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Pearson measures linear correlation on raw values
Spearman measures monotonic correlation on ranks
Pearson assumes normality; Spearman does not
Spearman is robust to outliers
Use cases for each

questions.question: Describe the various visualization techniques used for bivariate and multivariate data analysis.
keyPoints: Scatter plots for two numerical variables
Heatmaps for correlation matrices
Pair plots for all pairwise relationships
Parallel coordinates for high-dimensional data
Radar charts for profile comparison
When to use each technique

questions.question: What is multivariate data? Explain the challenges associated with multivariate data analysis and how visualization techniques address them.
keyPoints: Definition: three or more variables per observation
Challenges: visualization beyond 3D, curse of dimensionality, complex interactions
Parallel coordinates, heatmaps, dimensionality reduction for visualization
Importance in ML for feature understanding
